{{Infobox book
| name = Blackflame
| slug = cradle-03-blackflame
| precedes = [[Skysworn (novel)|Skysworn]]
| follows = [[Soulsmith (novel)|Soulsmith]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-03-blackflame.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2017
| isbn = 9781943363384
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 368
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|While training as a [[Soulsmith]] under [[Fisher Gesha]], [[Lindon]] learns the importance of patience after a lapse in awareness leads to a [[Remnant]] attack. As he struggles with his fundamentals, political tensions rise: [[Jai Long]], seeking vengeance for the death of [[Kral]], begins a rebellion against his own clan with the support of Chief [[Gokren]] and the [[Sandviper sect]]. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] is taken under the tutelage of Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius, an [[Underlord]] who introduces him to [[Yerin]] and the complexities of the escalating conflict between the Arelius family{{!}}Arelius and [[Jai]] clans. As the [[Jai clan]] begins seizing Arelius family{{!}}Arelius assets, [[Lindon]] commits to intensive training alongside [[Eithan]] and [[Yerin]] aboard a massive cloudship.

During their journey, [[Lindon]] practices physical conditioning and the [[Heaven and Earth Purification Wheel]] technique. Upon arriving at [[Serpent's Grave]], [[Eithan]] is revealed to be the [[Arelius Patriarch]], where he engages in a tense confrontation with [[Jai Daishou]], the Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch of the rival [[Jai clan]]. To prepare [[Lindon]] for the coming conflict, [[Eithan]] leads him to the Arelius family library{{!}}Arelius library, where [[Lindon]] chooses the destructive [[Path]] of [[Blackflame]]. To master this path, [[Eithan]] facilitates a dangerous contract between [[Lindon]] and [[Orthos]], a massive [[dragon]]-descendant turtle suffering from [[Blackflame]] buildup. This bond advances [[Lindon]] to the [[Jade stage]] and allows him to begin training under [[Orthos]]'s instruction.

[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] eventually undergo the ancient [[Blackflame Trials]]. After deciphering ancient [[scripts]] to learn new techniques, they face a series of increasingly difficult challenges: the [[Enforcer Trial]], the [[Striker Trial]], and the [[Ruler Trial]]. During these trials, [[Lindon]] discovers that his [[Sylvan Riverseed]] has evolved into a human-like form capable of cleansing [[Blackflame]] corrosion. However, the training is interrupted by the [[Jai clan]]'s massive mobilization. [[Jai Daishou]] launches a coordinated attack on [[Serpent's Grave]], trapping [[Eithan]] and plunging the city into war. Amidst the chaos, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] must fight through both enemy warriors and a maddened [[Orthos]].

In the heat of battle, [[Yerin]] advances to [[Highgold]] by utilizing her master's [[Remnant]], while [[Lindon]] survives a near-fatal encounter with [[Sandviper Gokren]] by using his evolved [[Sylvan Riverseed]] to stabilize his spirit, advancing him to [[Lowgold]]. Following the battle, [[Eithan]] reveals to [[Lindon]] that they both possess pure cores and tasks him with rescuing the captive [[Jai Chen]]. Using [[pure madra]], [[Lindon]] successfully repairs [[Jai Chen]]'s broken channels. As the conflict settles, the [[Abidan]] [[Suriel]] observes that [[Lindon]]'s presence is causing unpredictable deviations in [[fate]]. The story concludes with [[Lindon]] being taken into imperial custody by [[Skysworn]] [[Renfei]] and [[Bai Rou]], while [[Eithan]] meets with [[Emperor Naru Huan]] to discuss the implications of [[Lindon]]'s mastery over the [[Blackflame]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|While training as a [[Soulsmith]] under the stern [[Fisher Gesha]], [[Lindon]] successfully crafts a basic launcher construct from a dreadbeasts{{!}}dreadbeast's [[binding]] but is reprimanded for his overeager desire to skip fundamental steps. His focus on the future leads to a lapse in awareness, resulting in a sudden attack by a purple [[Remnant]] that leaves him vulnerable. Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha eventually intervenes to defeat the creature, using the experience to lecture [[Lindon]] on the dangers of carelessness and impatience.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Long]] reveals to his ailing sister that [[Kral]] was killed by an [[Iron disciple]] of the [[Arelius Underlord]], promising her he will use the [[Ancestor's Spear]] to seek vengeance. After gaining the unexpected support of Chief [[Gokren]] and the [[Sandviper sect]], [[Jai Long]] prepares to march against his own clan. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] returns to the [[Alliance camp]] with [[Fisher Gesha]], where he encounters Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius, who asserts his Authority{{!}}authority as an [[Underlord]] before taking [[Lindon]] for training.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]] leads [[Lindon]] to a newly constructed training hall where [[Yerin]] demonstrates her combat prowess using an intricate runic dummy system. After [[Lindon]] tests the equipment, [[Eithan]] introduces his relative [[Cassias]], who arrives with news that the [[Jai clan]] is aggressively seizing Arelius family{{!}}Arelius assets. The chapter concludes with [[Cassias]] revealing that the [[Jai clan]]'s recent aggressive maneuvers may have been triggered by an internal attack within their own ranks.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Long]] and the [[Sandviper sect]] ambush a [[Jai clan]] shelter, capturing several [[sacred artists]] to test the power-siphoning capabilities of the legendary [[Ancestor's Spear]]. The raid is interrupted by a [[Jai clan elder]] and his elite guards, leading to a violent confrontation where [[Jai Long]] uses the spear to kill several enemies and absorb a powerful [[Remnant]]. Despite the intense battle and the overwhelming power of his new weapon, [[Jai Long]] remains wary of Chief [[Gokren]]'s intentions as they prepare to depart.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|After practicing with training dummies, [[Lindon]] learns from [[Yerin]] that his [[Iron body]] allows him to train longer by masking fatigue. [[Eithan]] arrives and explains that the [[Jai clan]] is effectively declaring war on his family, forcing [[Lindon]] to decide whether to stay with the [[Arelius family]] despite the dangerous training ahead. Ultimately, [[Lindon]] chooses to commit to the path and joins [[Eithan]], [[Yerin]], and [[Fisher Gesha]] aboard a massive flying cloudship for a month of intensive training.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Long]] decides to take his sister, [[Jai Chen]], on a journey west to avoid the [[Jai clan]]'s retaliation, aided by [[Gokren]] and his sect. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] travels on a [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]] with [[Eithan]], [[Cassias]], [[Yerin]], and Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha, using the time to train his physical strength and [[Soulsmithing]] skills. During the journey, [[Eithan]] teaches [[Lindon]] the difficult [[Heaven and Earth Purification Wheel]] cycling technique to help expand his core capacity.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|As the [[Sky's Mercy]] approaches [[Serpent's Grave]], [[Cassias Arelius]] discusses [[Eithan]]'s ambitious and controversial plan to train [[Lindon]] using a [[dragon]]'s [[aura]]. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] engage in intense training sessions where [[Eithan]] critiques their progress and [[Yerin]]'s struggle to utilize her master's [[Remnant]]. The chapter concludes as the ship arrives at [[Shiryu Mountain]], with [[Eithan]] privately noting a significant realization regarding [[Lindon]]'s mysterious origins.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] arrive at [[Serpent's Grave]], a massive city built within the skeletal remains of [[dragons]], where Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius is revealed to be the family's Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch. After receiving reports of various local misfortunes, [[Eithan]] engages in a tense verbal confrontation with [[Jai Daishou]], the Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch of the rival [[Jai clan]], who uses his power to physically suppress everyone in the courtyard. Following the encounter, [[Eithan]] sends [[Yerin]] to a refinery for an elixirs{{!}}elixir and prepares to take [[Lindon]] to the [[Arelius family library]].}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]] shows [[Lindon]] a copper [[construct]] that serves as the [[Arelius family]]'s knowledge repository, using it to demonstrate various [[sacred arts]] through crimson and green mannequins. To prepare [[Lindon]] for his upcoming fight against [[Jai Long]], [[Eithan]] presents several potential [[Paths]], eventually leading [[Lindon]] to choose the destructive [[Path]] of [[Blackflame]]. The chapter concludes with [[Eithan]] revealing that they can practice this new [[Path]] by accessing a sealed [[dragon]] descendant located in an underground chamber.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]] leads [[Lindon]] to a cave to meet [[Orthos]], a massive [[dragon]]-descendant turtle suffering from an overwhelming buildup of [[Blackflame madra]]. To help both the beast and [[Lindon]], [[Eithan]] facilitates a dangerous contract that bonds their cores, successfully advancing [[Lindon]] to the [[Jade stage]] while providing [[Orthos]] relief. While [[Eithan]] secretly enhances [[Lindon's Sylvan Riverseed]], he enters a trial by combat with [[Orthos]] to settle the terms of [[Lindon]]'s upcoming training.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Orthos]] teaches [[Lindon]] about [[vital aura]] and the nature of advancement before instructing him to cycle [[Blackflame aura]] for three days near a dragon's skeleton. Meanwhile, [[Jai Long]] executes a calculated ambush on the [[Jai clan]] to further his own agenda, and [[Yerin]] undergoes combat training with [[Eithan]]. Finally, [[Eithan]] prepares [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] to undergo the ancient [[Blackflame Trials]] together, revealing his ambitious but uncertain vision for the family's future.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] settle into a campsite near the [[Blackflame Trials]], where they discover large crabs inhabiting the nearby cliffs. After passing through a red archway, [[Lindon]] uses his knowledge of ancient Script{{!}}script to decipher the history of the Blackflame{{!}}Blackflames and learns a new [[Enforcer technique]] called '[[Burning Cloak]].' As [[Lindon]] activates the trial via a crystal ball, [[Eithan]] brings [[Cassias]] to a hidden temple that serves as the control center for the trials, where [[Eithan]] convinces [[Cassias]] to oversee and intensify the challenges facing the two youths.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] attempts to navigate a trial by outrunning stone soldiers, but he eventually fails when his [[Blackflame core]] exhausts itself. After the trial ends, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] recover from their battle, with [[Yerin]] theorizing that the constructs were actually a powerful [[Forger technique]]. Meanwhile, in another realm, an [[Abidan]] named [[Suriel]] discovers a hidden facility containing millions of preserved people and receives a fragmented, chaotic message from her friend Ozriel regarding a quarantine plan for corrupted worlds.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|After discovering that his [[Sylvan Riverseed]] has transformed into a more complex, human-like form with the ability to cleanse madra channels of [[Blackflame]] corrosion, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] attempt to exploit this new advantage. They challenge the [[Enforcer Trial]] twice in a single day, pushing the course to its limits and nearly reaching the exit before [[Cassias]] intervenes by reinforcing the trial's soldiers. Despite the increased difficulty and their own exhaustion, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] prepare for a third consecutive attempt.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|After successfully completing the [[Enforcer Trial]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] begin practicing the [[Striker technique]], which involves projecting powerful bursts of [[madra]]. Their training is interrupted by [[Eithan]]'s sudden arrival, where he offers [[Yerin]] a [[Spirit Manifestation pill]] and promises [[Lindon]] [[Blackflame]] [[Truegold scales]] as rewards for future progress. The chapter concludes with the duo entering the [[Striker Trial]], facing a defensive [[aura]] wall that launches stone spears at them while they attempt to destroy mysterious shadowy targets.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] successfully completes the [[Striker Trial]] by using pure madra shells to stabilize his [[Blackflame]], after receiving instruction from the erratic [[dragon]]-turtle [[Orthos]]. Meanwhile, [[Jai Daishou]], the [[Underlord]] of the [[Jai clan]], captures the rebel [[Jai Long]] and his sister, forcing him to swear absolute loyalty. The chapter concludes with the [[Underlord]] revealing a grand plan to strike against the [[Arelius family]], signaling a massive mobilization of the [[Jai clan]]'s forces across [[Serpent's Grave]].}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Jai clan]] launches a coordinated, violent attack on the [[Arelius family]] and their allies throughout the city, trapping [[Eithan]] behind a Boundary formations{{!}}boundary formation while [[Cassias]] fights through enemy Highgold{{!}}Highgolds to reach his family. Amidst the urban chaos, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] are interrupted during their [[Ruler Trial]] by a maddened [[Orthos]], who crashes into their location in a state of uncontrollable rage. [[Lindon]] attempts to use a halfsilver{{!}}halfsilver dagger to subdue the sacred beast, but the weapon snaps against [[Orthos]]'s thick skin, forcing him to continue fighting alongside [[Yerin]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|After a grueling battle with the sacred beast [[Orthos]], [[Yerin]] and [[Lindon]] decide to spare its life, only to emerge from the [[Ruler]] [[Trials]] to find [[Serpent's Grave]] engulfed in war. While attempting to escape, [[Yerin]] is intercepted by two [[Truegold]] Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper warriors, and [[Lindon]] is brutally attacked by a Sandvipers{{!}}Sandviper elder. As [[Lindon]] lies dying and depleted of [[madra]], he discovers a mysterious glowing marble in the dirt, only to be interrupted by the sudden return of [[Orthos]].}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]] advances to [[Highgold]] by crushing her master's [[Remnant]] to fuel a massive surge of [[sword aura]], allowing her to hold her own in a fierce duel against the [[Truegold]] [[Jai Long]]. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] survives a life-threatening encounter with [[Sandviper Gokren]] and [[Orthos]] by using the [[Sylvan Riverseed]] to stabilize his spirit, successfully advancing to [[Lowgold]]. Following the battle, [[Lindon]] and a recovering [[Orthos]] head toward the signal of [[Eithan]]'s power to find [[Yerin]].}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius survives a devastating assault by the [[Jai Patriarch]], [[Jai Daishou]], using an unexpected combination of [[pure madra]] and mundane tools like a broom and tailor's scissors. After the battle, [[Eithan]] reveals to [[Lindon]] that they both possess pure cores and shares mysterious glass marbles with him, before tasking [[Lindon]] with rescuing [[Jai Chen]]. [[Lindon]] infiltrates the [[Jai]] estate using an Arelius family{{!}}Arelius emblem and encounters the captive [[Jai Chen]], who is struggling with a compromised spirit.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Lindon{{!}}Wei Shi Lindon uses a miniature water Remnant and [[pure madra]] to repair [[Jai Chen]]'s broken channels, restoring her ability to cycle [[madra]]. After the procedure, [[Lindon]] departs the [[Jai clan]], leaving [[Jai Chen]] with a sense of profound debt. Meanwhile, in the void, the [[Judge Suriel]] observes the destruction of [[Iterations]] and realizes that [[Lindon]]'s presence has caused significant, unpredictable deviations in [[fate]], prompting a summons from [[Makiel]].}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Skysworn]] [[Renfei]] and [[Bai Rou]] discover a young [[Blackflame]] user named Lindon{{!}}Wei Shi Lindon practicing the [[Ruler Trial]] at Shiryu Mountain{{!}}Mount Shiryu, leading them to take him into imperial custody. Meanwhile, Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius visits [[Emperor Naru Huan]] to reveal that he has killed a [[Jai Underlord]] and admits to teaching [[Lindon]] the [[Path of Black Flame]]. Despite [[the Emperor]]'s concerns regarding the return of the Blackflame{{!}}Blackflames, [[Eithan]] requests that [[Lindon]] be allowed to continue fighting.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Long]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Orthos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Cassias]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Fisher Gesha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Daishou]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Gokren]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai Chen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sword Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Presence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Ozriel (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Emperor (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Cassias Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jing]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Little Blue]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sylvan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Arelius Underlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Naru Huan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Naru Cassias Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Renfei]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Arelius family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jai clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Serpent's Grave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sacred Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Desolate Wilds]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sandvipers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Blackflame family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Shiryu Mountain]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Wei clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Naru clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[sacred artists]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Five Factions Alliance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Transcendent Ruins]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Limit]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Naru]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Cloud Hammer School]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Empire (Cradle)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Blackflame madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Bloodforged Iron body]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Heaven and Earth Purification Wheel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Burning Cloak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Goldsign]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Path of Black Flame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Stellar Spear]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Empty Palm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Forged madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Striker technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Enforcer technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Enforce]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Ruler technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[sword aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Stellar Spear madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Star Lance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Path of Grasping Sky]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[vital aura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Copper sight]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[soulfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Void Dragon’s Dance]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Remnant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sylvan Riverseed]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Ancestor’s Spear]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Sky's Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Ancestor's Spear]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[drudge]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Four Corners Rotation Pill]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|spider-construct}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin's Goldsign]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|red rope}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[dream tablets]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Spirit Manifestation pill]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Highgold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Soulsmith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Underlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[pure madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Blackflame Trials]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Iron]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lowgold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Truegold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Blackflame core]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Jade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Skysworn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[construct]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Riverseed]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Striker Trial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Ruler Trial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Striker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Forger]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Path of Twin Stars]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Soulsmiths]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL22094140W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
